methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $500M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Wandelbots, a Series G robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $126M to date, operates within a sector represented by one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The narrow peer set at identical or adjacent stage limits reliance on median multiples.

Given insufficient same-stage comparables (0 direct matches, 1 adjacent-stage peer identified), peer-median triangulation is not applicable. The analysis instead relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement to a $500M base derived from total capital raised. This yields an implied valuation of $625M.

The triangulated range supported by available methodology spans $531M (low) to $719M (high), with a median point of $625M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty: the absence of granular same-stage peer data limits confidence in the step-up multiplier's applicability to Wandelbots specifically, and the $500M base valuation itself represents a proxy assumption rather than observed transaction data. Investors should regard this range as preliminary pending additional comparable transaction evidence or updated company financials that would enable DCF or revenue-multiple validation.
